tex:
\begin{question*}{12}{a}One day, Mahika records the number of teachers and students who cycle to school.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|}
\hline
& Tally & Frequency \\
\hline
Teachers & |||| & \\
\hline
Students & |||| |||| |||| |||| ||| & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\begin{subpartsaliged}
Complete the frequency column in the table.

Work out the percentage of people who cycle that are students.

\end{subpartsaliged}

(b)Mahika records how 120 students from Year 1 and Year 2 travel to school.\\
\indentEach student walks, cycles or travels by bus.
(a) 48 students are in Year 1.

(b) 77 students walk.

(c) 5 students in Year 2 cycle.

(d) 36 students travel by bus.

(e) $\frac{4}{9}$ of the students who travel by bus are in Year 1.

\begin{subpartsaliged}
Complete the table.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
& Walk & Cycle & Bus & Total \\
\hline
Year 1 & & & & \\
\hline
Year 2 & & & & \\
\hline
Total & & & & 120 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

One of the 120 students is chosen at random.\\
 Work out the probability that this student does not travel by bus to school.

\end{subpartsaliged}

(c)There have been 24 complaints about one of the buses.\\
\indentThe complaints are:
(a) The bus is late.

(b) The price is too high.

(c) The bus is crowded.

\begin{subpartsaliged}
Complete the table.

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|c|}
\hline
Complaint & Frequency & Pie chart sector angle \\
\hline
Late & 10 & \\
\hline
Price & 6 & \\
\hline
Crowded & 8 & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Complete the pie chart.

%\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

\end{subpartsaliged}

\end{question*}

\begin{question*}{9}{a}
%\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

The diagram shows a fair 9-sided spinner.\\
The numbers on the spinner are 2, 3, 5, 5, 5, 6, 6, 7 and 8.

\begin{subpartsaliged}
The spinner is spun once.

Write down the probability that the spinner lands on
    (a) the number 8,

    

(b) a number less than 7.

    

The spinner is spun 135 times.

Work out the expected number of times the spinner lands on the number 6.

\end{subpartsaliged}

(b)Hitesh throws a dice 80 times.\\
\indentThe results are shown in the table.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Number thrown & Frequency \\
\hline
1 & 15 \\
2 & 16 \\
3 & 14 \\
4 & 11 \\
5 & 9 \\
6 & 15 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\begin{subpartsaliged}
Write down the mode.

Work out the range.

Work out the median.

Calculate the mean.

\end{subpartsaliged}

\end{question*}

tex:
\begin{question*}{13}{a}Tarak has two fields.\\
He grows wheat, barley and corn in his fields.

The diagram shows Tarak's two triangular fields, PQR and PRS.\\
Angle $RPS = 90^{\circ}$ and angle $PRS = 53^{\circ}$.\\
$PQ = 174 \text{ m}$, $QR = 120 \text{ m}$ and $PR = 126 \text{ m}$.

%\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

\begin{subpartsaliged}
Show that angle $PRQ = 90^{\circ}$.

Calculate the area of the quadrilateral PQRS.
Give your answer correct to 4 significant figures.

\end{subpartsaliged}

(b)
\begin{subpartsaliged}
The mass, $m$ tonnes, of wheat grown in 2021 is 4.3 tonnes, correct to 1 decimal place.

Complete this statement about the value of $m$.

{...} $\le m <$ ][]

In 2020, 2.6 tonnes of barley is grown.
In 2021, 3.25 tonnes of barley is grown.

Show that the percentage increase in barley grown from 2020 to 2021 is 25\%.

In 2019, 2.4 tonnes of corn is grown.
In 2020, 20\% more corn is grown than in 2019.
In 2021, 20\% less corn is grown than in 2020.

Calculate the amount of corn grown in 2021.

\end{subpartsaliged}

\end{question*}
